nuggets.text: Colossyan Free is a trial-style entry point with 3 video minutes, 15 scenes, 3-minute max video length, 20 plus stock avatars, and watermark included.
Starter begins around $27 monthly or $19 billed annually and includes up to 15 video minutes per month, 5-minute max video length, 70 plus stock avatars, and 3 instant avatars.
Starter also includes unlimited viewers, multiple-avatar scenes, one voice clone, 1080p download resolution, and watermark removal.
Business begins around $88 monthly or $70 billed annually, adds unlimited video minutes, 170 plus stock avatars, 10 instant avatars per editor, and 4K download resolution.
Business also adds up to 3 seats, 4 interactive videos per month, and 10 auto-translations per month for multilingual rollout.
Enterprise adds unlimited access to interactive quizzes, branching scenarios, auto-translations, and more, alongside 200 plus stock avatars.
The Enterprise plan explicitly lists brand kits, SAML and SSO, and SCORM export for LMS-aligned deployment.
Colossyan positions language support at over 70 languages and accents with over 100 voices across the plan matrix.
The pricing page includes AI script assistant, prompt-to-video, AI image generation, voice upload, pronunciation dictionary, and branded templates as part of the workflow story.
The same plan details highlight shared workspaces, team permissions, team templates, custom share pages, HTML embedding, subtitles, PPT or PDF import, and in-app screen recording.

topUpClaims.note: Official refund policy says paid self-serve plans can qualify for a full refund within 14 days if usage remains below the documented threshold, while Enterprise plans and heavier usage do not qualify in the same way.

Official pricing clearly distinguishes the usage ladder across plans, including trial-style free access, capped self-serve minutes on Starter, broader interactivity and translation allowances on Business, and enterprise governance on higher tiers.
Official pricing directly supports plan-gated export differences such as Full HD download on Starter, 4K on Business, and SCORM export on Enterprise-oriented tiers.

Official terms support a bounded ownership model: each party retains its own IP, customer-created content can be published after the subscription term, and customers remain responsible for third-party rights, consents, and avatar-related permissions.

Official pricing supports parts of the access and visibility model through shared workspaces, team permissions, custom share pages, and enterprise governance features, but it does not by itself define a full default visibility policy for every asset type.
